tool_input.summary: Home Plate silica at Gusev (MER Spirit) is astrobiologically important because opaline silica can form in hydrothermal/fumarolic or hot-spring settings that provide chemical energy, strong redox/temperature/pH gradients, and rapid mineral precipitation that can entomb and preserve textures and organic matter—making it both a potentially habitable environment and a high-fidelity biosignature preservation medium, not just evidence of water-rock interaction. Relevant PDS holdings for reanalyzing Spirit’s mineralogical/chemical context include Mini-TES (silica identification), APXS (bulk chemistry), and Pancam (geologic/stratigraphic and spectral context).

candidates.title: MER2 Mini-TES Derived Emissivity Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Mini-TES derived emissivity is the key dataset type used to diagnose opaline silica via thermal-IR spectral features in Spirit’s Home Plate region (MER2 = Spirit).

candidates.title: MER2 M MTES 4 EMR V1
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 version of MER2 Mini-TES derived emissivity volumes; useful if you need the legacy archive structure/labels cited by older Home Plate silica papers.

candidates.title: MER APXS Derived Oxide Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: APXS derived oxide chemistry constrains silica enrichment, leaching/acid-sulfate alteration vs hydrothermal sinter scenarios, and associated element mobility relevant to habitability and preservation.

candidates.title: MER2 Pancam Science Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated Pancam multispectral imaging provides stratigraphic/geomorphic context at Home Plate and can map spectral units associated with silica-rich soils/outcrops.

candidates.title: MER2-M-PANCAM-3-RADCAL-SCI-V2.0
candidates.reasoning: PDS3 calibrated Pancam archive; useful for reproducing earlier Home Plate unit maps and tying them to published Spirit traverse products.

You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Why do the Home Plate silica deposits matter for astrobiology, beyond simply indicating water-rock interaction?
